location: 02-spec.md decision #6 + 'Verified contract - What a captured item IS' + REQ-008; vs C:/dev/Orky/plugin/feedback/feedback.js:123-148 (fileChannel: emit->outbox, pull<-inbox), cli.js:74-89 (apply pulls the INBOX), feedback.js:312-343 (applyItems keys on item.kind + top-level title/detail)
claim: The spec's D5 grounding pins a wrong upstream fact - the exact expensive failure mode this review targets. 'feedback emit' writes the work.request EVENT to the project's feedback OUTBOX (plugin->control-plane direction), while the orchestrator's 'apply' pulls the INBOX (ch.pull() reads <dir>/inbox/); nothing in shipped Orky bridges an emitted outbox event into the inbox applyItems consumes (F7's own spec, 0007 02-spec.md:445-447, explicitly deferred that 'outbox-event -> inbox-item' mapping to 'Orky's own transport', which exists only as the separate http control-plane web app - not for file mode; the shapes don't even align: event {type:'work.request', payload:{title,detail}} vs inbox item {kind:'work.request', title, detail}, so applyItems would skip a naively-copied event as 'unsupported kind undefined'). Consequently, for a file-mode project (the default local case, and the mode 00-intake.md itself names), a captured item sits in the outbox with NO shipped path to pull/apply/.orky-backlog.jsonl/planner triage - yet REQ-008 MANDATES success copy presenting it as 'captured/queued to the project's Orky inbox for later triage', and decision #6 pins 'Captured - queued in <root>'s Orky inbox for triage' as 'Ground truth (Verified contract)'. That copy is wrong twice (it is the OUTBOX, not the inbox; and 'for triage' promises a consumer that does not exist in file mode), so REQ-008's mandated wording contradicts D5/CONV-013's own MUST that the result be presented as 'what it is'. The Verified-contract sentence 'Only the orchestrator's later apply ... turns a pulled work.request into a .orky/backlog.jsonl record' is accurate in isolation but is mispinned as the fate of THE CAPTURED ITEM, silently conflating the emitted outbox event with a pulled inbox item.
fix: Re-ground decision #6 and REQ-008 on the verified emit semantics: the success copy may claim only what emit does ('captured to <root>'s Orky feedback outbox' / 'durably recorded for the Orky control plane'), never 'inbox' and never an unconditional 'for triage'. Add the transport gap to 'Upstream inconsistencies flagged' (file-mode outbox work.request events have no shipped consumer; the event->inbox mapping lives only in the http control-plane) and escalate upstream (Orky feedback plugin) whether locally-emitted work.request events should be routed to apply (e.g. emit-to-inbox for self-injection, or an apply that also drains own-outbox work.requests) - do not let F12's copy paper over it.
resolution: Re-verified 2026-07-02 against revision 1 + shipped source in BOTH repos. Upstream repaired the root cause: plugin v0.28.0 ships 'feedback submit'/submitItem (feedback.js:278-299, cli.js:71-88), the sanctioned local-inbox injection path, and REQ-013 amends F7's doSubmitWork to ride it. Ground truth established by EXECUTING the full chain in a scratch .orky (file mode): submit --json wrote inbox/IN-729047a23341.json; apply pulled it and applyItems->backlogAdd produced WORK-9d8e9810b552 status:'pending' in .orky/backlog.jsonl with title/detail byte-verbatim (including a '--json'-prefixed title). Decision #6's copy 'Captured - queued in <root>'s Orky inbox for triage' is now literally true end-to-end; the old outbox dead-end flag is correctly retired in 'Upstream inconsistencies flagged'. REQ-013's supersession inventory of the six emit-era dispatcher pins was independently re-derived by grepping both dispatcher suites and is complete for assertions (see FINDING-007 for a prose-only residue).

location: 02-spec.md 'Verified contract - What a captured item IS' (file/noop only) + REQ-008; vs C:/dev/Orky/plugin/feedback/feedback.js:48-53 (mode inferred 'http' when endpoint set), :150-175 (httpChannel.emit: ANY failure -> spool to outbox, returns {sent:false, spooled:true} while the CLI still prints ok:true, mode:'http')
claim: REQ-008 keys success on ok && dispatched for EVERY result shape, but the Verified contract enumerates only file mode and noop - it omits that a tracked project's .orky/config.json can select http mode (or imply it via 'endpoint'), where emit returns ok:true/mode:'http' in TWO sub-shapes F7 maps identically to {ok:true, dispatched:true, feedback:'enabled'}: (a) sent:true - the event was POSTed to a remote control plane (nothing exists in <root>'s local feedback dir at all), and (b) sent:false/spooled:true - the POST failed and the event sits in a local retry spool that NO shipped code ever retries (the control-plane spec's pseudocode names channel.flushOutbox(), but feedback.js exports no flush/retry). F12 rightly MUST NOT branch on data.mode, which means the one success copy must be TRUE across all three shapes - the spec never established that universe, so its 'confirmed against shipped source' claim is incomplete for the exact field the success gate keys on.
fix: Extend the Verified contract to enumerate every ok&&dispatched emit shape (file sent, http sent, http spooled) and require the success copy to be truthful across all of them (e.g. 'captured' without asserting a specific resting place or delivery); note the absent spool-retry as a flagged upstream limitation alongside the existing F7 flags.
resolution: Re-verified 2026-07-02 by EXECUTING every submit shape against cli.js: file-mode success (exit 0, {ok:true,mode:'file',id:'IN-…',kind,path} - the ONLY success shape), disabled refusal (exit 1, {ok:false,mode:'noop',error:'feedback is disabled — …ADR-027)'}), http refusal (exit 1, mode:'http' redirect - endpoint-implied http config REFUSES, never spools), validation refusal (exit 1, mode:'file'), internal error (exit 2, {error} via cli.js:153-156, incl. malformed --json), old-plugin analogue (unknown subcommand: 0 stdout bytes, exit 2 -> cli-unparseable '(0 bytes)'). mode:'noop' is UNIQUE to the disabled refusal among exit-1 shapes (feedback.js:281 vs :284/:288/:292), so REQ-013's dispatcher discrimination is sound; mapCliRunToResult:78-83 confirmed surfacing every nonzero-exit parseable shape as cli-error with parsed.error verbatim. The emit-era sent/spooled ambiguity is genuinely gone from the success keying, and the spool-no-retry residual flag is retained and confirmed true (no flush/retry exported anywhere in the plugin). Universe complete; no shape missed.

location: 02-spec.md REQ-002 (acceptance 'all eight testids render in the open form state' vs normative 'orky-capture-error, rendered only on a failure - REQ-009')
claim: REQ-002 contradicts itself (and REQ-009). Its normative text defines orky-capture-error as 'the in-modal result region (rendered only on a failure - REQ-009)', but its acceptance demands 'all eight testids render in the open form state' - and the enumerated eight include orky-capture-error. A test implementing the acceptance literally forces the error region to render in a freshly-opened, failure-free form, violating the same REQ's MUST and REQ-009's failure-only rendering; an implementation satisfying the MUST fails the acceptance. The tests phase cannot satisfy both readings.
fix: Amend REQ-002's acceptance to: the seven non-error testids render in the open form state; orky-capture-error renders only under a mocked ok:false result (cross-reference REQ-009's vectors) and is absent at open.
resolution: Re-verified 2026-07-02: REQ-002's amended acceptance now demands exactly the SEVEN non-error testids at open, orky-capture-error ABSENT there, rendering only under a mocked ok:false (cross-referencing REQ-009's per-kind vectors) and disappearing on a successful retry - matching the normative failure-only MUST. The 'Key testids' block and decision #5 are consistent (eight-name NAMESPACE inventory, not a render-at-open claim). Contradiction gone; both readings now satisfiable by one implementation.

location: 02-spec.md REQ-006/REQ-012 acceptance; vs src/renderer/main.tsx:6-7 (app ships under <StrictMode>) and 0009 FINDING-036 (dev double effect invocation consumed guard evidence and broke a pinned fetch count)
claim: REQ-006's zero-dispatch/single-flight and REQ-012's unmount-mid-flight vectors invoke CONV-031 ('real component mount/unmount') but never require the vectors to run under StrictMode, even though main.tsx wraps the entire app in <StrictMode> and this project has already shipped exactly this failure class (F9 FINDING-036: StrictMode's mount->unmount->remount double-invokes effects and consumed the wasHidden evidence, breaking a pinned count). A plain-mount harness passes an implementation that issues the dispatch from an effect keyed on a submit-requested flag (double-fired on the StrictMode remount -> two work items from one gesture in the shipped dev tree) and cannot see whether the in-flight/single-flight guard survives the synthetic remount. The spec also never pins that the orkySubmitWork call is issued synchronously from the gesture handler rather than an effect - the one structural property that makes the class impossible.
fix: Add to REQ-006/REQ-012 acceptance: the mount/zero-dispatch, single-flight, and unmount-mid-flight vectors are additionally exercised with the component wrapped in <StrictMode> (mount-unmount-remount), still asserting exactly zero/one dispatch; and add a structural pin that the api.orkySubmitWork call site lives in an event handler, not inside a useEffect. propose CONV: A count/single-flight discipline on a component that ships inside a StrictMode-wrapped tree MUST include a StrictMode (double mount/effect invocation) vector, not only a plain real-lifecycle mount.
resolution: Re-verified 2026-07-02: main.tsx:6-7 <StrictMode> wrapper confirmed in source. REQ-006's amended acceptance runs the zero-dispatch-on-mount and single-flight vectors under <StrictMode> (still exactly zero/one), REQ-012 runs zero-IPC-on-mount and unmount-mid-flight under <StrictMode>, and REQ-006 + the Public interface pin the structural property (orkySubmitWork issued synchronously from the gesture handler; no useEffect body/callee in F12 files references it). The vectors are real: React's dev build (which vitest uses) double-invokes effects under StrictMode, so the double-dispatch failure mode is exercisable exactly as specified. Proposed convention recorded (spec 'Proposed conventions' #2).

location: 02-spec.md decision #4 + REQ-003 (additive props: ariaLabel?/z? only); vs src/renderer/components/OrkyRootPicker.tsx:64 (aria-label 'Bind Orky pane to a tracked project'), :66 (visible header 'Bind to a tracked Orky project'), :80-83 (bind-oriented empty copy)
claim: Decision #4 relabels ONLY the accessible name (ariaLabel 'Capture work for a tracked Orky project') while the picker's VISIBLE strings stay byte-identical in the capture flow - no prop exists for them - so sighted users invoking capture see a dialog headed 'Bind to a tracked Orky project' (and empty-state copy about tracking projects to BIND) while screen-reader users hear a capture-specific name. The visible copy actively mis-describes the action (nothing is being bound to a pane), and the accessible name diverging from the visible heading breaks label/visible-text coherence. No frozen test pins the header or aria-label literal (verified: TEST-434 pins testids/registryError/track-copy/Escape only; no e2e pins the label), so a default-preserving visible-heading prop is available under the same additive-only discipline - the spec simply stopped one prop short.
fix: Extend decision #4/REQ-003's additive prop set with an optional visible heading/title prop (default: the existing F9 header, byte-identical when omitted), require the capture flow to pass a capture-specific heading consistent with its ariaLabel, and add an acceptance vector that the capture flow's picker shows capture-appropriate visible copy. propose CONV: When a reused dialog gains a flow-specific accessible name, its visible heading MUST be configurable in the same change (defaults preserved) so the screen-reader name and the visible copy can never diverge.
resolution: Re-verified 2026-07-02: decision #4, REQ-003, and the Public interface add the optional heading? prop with the byte-identical default 'Bind to a tracked Orky project' (OrkyRootPicker.tsx:66 verified; ariaLabel default :64 verified); the capture flow MUST pass BOTH a capture-specific ariaLabel AND heading, and the acceptance asserts the visible header matches /capture/i and NOT /bind/i together with the aria-label so name/heading coherence is a tested property. Additive-safety re-verified: TEST-434 (orky-pane-structure.test.ts:120-133) pins testids/registryError/registrySnapshot//track/i/Escape/not-registryRoots( only - neither literal; repo-wide grep finds no other test pinning either string; the heading prop adds no TEST-433 banned string. The empty-state copy (:79-83) is tracking-oriented and flow-neutral, so the heading+ariaLabel repair fully closes the divergence. Proposed convention recorded (spec 'Proposed conventions' #3).

location: 02-spec.md REQ-011 acceptance (the no-consumer sweep grep); vs C:/dev/Termhalla/.orky/baseline/architecture.md:118
claim: REQ-011's acceptance pins: rg 'no renderer UI consumes|no renderer consumer' over src/**, docs/**, CLAUDE.md, .orky/baseline/** 'finds only the updated phrasing'. But .orky/baseline/architecture.md:118 matches that pattern with a claim that stays TRUE after F12 ('the mutation surface (registry:addRoot/removeRoot) still has no renderer consumer' - a REGISTRY claim, not an orkyAction claim; F12 never touches the mutation surface). A test implementing the acceptance literally must either go red on a correct file or force an edit that falsifies a true baseline statement.
fix: Scope the acceptance's grep to the orkyAction claim class (e.g. anchor the pattern on the orkyAction/write-capable context, or explicitly allowlist the registry-mutation line in architecture.md:118 as a distinct, still-true claim).
resolution: Re-verified 2026-07-02: REQ-011's amended acceptance scopes the sweep to the orkyAction claim class and explicitly allowlists the registry-mutation line at architecture.md:117-118, requiring it byte-unchanged and asserting it present. Ran the sweep pattern over the exact scope: the universe is exactly TWO matches - ipc-contract.ts:179 (the orkyAction no-consumer claim REQ-011 updates) and architecture.md:118 (the allowlisted, still-true registry claim) - so 'finds ONLY (i) updated phrasings + (ii) the unmodified registry line' is implementable and correct as written.

location: 02-spec.md REQ-013 + the 'Upstream fit' additive-safe classification of TEST-290; vs C:/dev/Termhalla/tests/shared/orky-action-validate.test.ts:322 (it-description: title/detail/phase 'travel only inside the JSON-encoded --payload argument, never as their own raw argv element') and :41 (the suite's scope comment saying the same)
claim: The REQ-013 repair (emit -> submit) leaves a stale-claim residue its own CONV-008 machinery misses: after the amendment, submitWork free-text fields travel inside the '--json' argv element (cli.js:76), not '--payload' - yet frozen TEST-290's it-description and the validate suite's :41 scope comment still state the '--payload' mechanism as the RATIONALE for accepting '--'-prefixed titles. The assertions stay green (the validator is byte-unchanged, and the safety property genuinely carries over to --json), but the frozen prose becomes a false mechanism claim of exactly the class CONV-008 bans - and because tests/shared/orky-action-validate.test.ts is frozen and NOT in the enumerated supersession inventory, the tests phase has no sanctioned way to correct it. Root cause: the revision-1 argv-pin grep (the work.request / --payload / --type pattern) was scoped to tests/main/** only, so tests/shared prose was never swept, while the spec DID enumerate the exactly-analogous header comment at orky-action-dispatcher.test.ts:37 for CONV-008 honesty - an inconsistency in the inventory's own standard.
fix: Add a prose-only seventh entry to REQ-011/REQ-013's supersession inventory: amend TEST-290's it-description and the :41 scope comment to name the '--json' element for submitWork (resolveEscalation keeps --payload), assertions byte-unchanged - or explicitly record the staleness as accepted with rationale. Also widen the argv-pin grep scope from tests/main/** to tests/** so test prose in tests/shared is inside the CONV-008 sweep. propose CONV: A CONV-008 stale-claim sweep triggered by changing a mechanism MUST include test files' descriptions/comments (tests/**), not only src/docs/baseline - a frozen test whose prose states the old mechanism is a stale doc like any other.
resolution: Functionally repaired at the tests phase (validate.test.ts scope comment + TEST-290 it-title now name the --json element; assertions byte-untouched). The spec-text mismatch this created is FINDING-020's subject — tracked there.

claim: Stale capture failure survives draft/root changes: failure state clears only when a NEW submit starts — editing title/detail or selecting a different project after a failed result leaves the previous orky-capture-error rendered (old data-error-kind + old CLI message) against a now-different pending request, outside REQ-009's draft-preserving retry semantics and conflicting with REQ-004's change-project recovery path. TEST-506 pins retry-without-editing only.
fix: Clear the failure when title/detail mutate or a different root is selected post-failure (or snapshot the failed request and hide the error when the draft no longer matches); add failure->edit and failure->change-project regressions
location: src/renderer/components/OrkyCaptureModal.tsx:55,69-70,93-97,175-193
verified_by: codex-cross-check (filed by coordinator; codex sandbox lock EPERM)
resolution: Re-verified 2026-07-02 (rev-2): failure clears on any draft edit (OrkyCaptureModal.tsx onTitleChange:68 / onDetailChange:69) and on selecting a DIFFERENT root (handlePickSelect:61 'if (picked !== root) setFailure(null)'), so a stale verdict never outlives its subject; draft untouched (REQ-009). Regressions pinned e2e: TEST-525 (draft-edit clears the region) + TEST-528 (Change project to a different root clears it, draft preserved).

claim: REQ-010 forbids any client-side length cap or truncation on title/detail, and REQ-013 sends the whole item as ONE JSON argv element via feedback submit --json. runOrkyCli (unchanged) maps EVERY non-numeric-exit-code execFile error -- a genuine 15s timeout, an abort, AND a spawn-level failure such as an oversized command line (Windows CreateProcess has roughly a 32767-character total command-line limit; an ENAMETOOLONG/E2BIG-class spawn error is exactly what an unbounded --json element can trigger) -- into the single shape exitCode:null, timedOut:true. mapCliRunToResult (byte-unchanged) checks run.timedOut FIRST and unconditionally returns errorKind cli-timeout, which OrkyCaptureModal.tsx renders as the INDETERMINATE state (the item may or may not have been captured, retrying may create a duplicate, per CONV-015). A spawn failure that never executed the child process cannot have written anything, so presenting it as indeterminate is a false claim -- it directly contradicts this feature's own security-tagged result-honesty goal (REQ-008/REQ-009) and 02-spec.md's own Upstream inconsistencies flagged note, which asserts an oversized payload surfaces as cli-error/cli-unparseable, honestly, not silently; that claim does not hold against the actual runOrkyCli implementation. TEST-471 only exercises a FAKE runCli with a 10000-line detail (JSON-serialization correctness only), so the real OS-level spawn-failure classification is completely untested. F12 is the first renderer-reachable surface that lets a human paste an arbitrarily large detail into this argv element -- submitWork previously had zero UI consumers (0007 TEST-282) -- so this latent gap in shared, unmodified code becomes newly and easily reachable through ordinary use (e.g. pasting a large log excerpt).
fix: Distinguish a genuine timeout from a spawn-level failure inside runOrkyCli (e.g. inspect the execFile error for a spawn-class code such as ENAMETOOLONG/EINVAL versus an actual elapsed-time-vs-timeoutMs timeout/abort) and map spawn failures to a definite cli-error/cli-unparseable outcome rather than the indeterminate cli-timeout; add a real (non-mocked) boundary test that drives an oversized detail through the actual runOrkyCli/execFile path to pin the true OS behavior, since TEST-471 only proves JSON-serialization correctness, not the runner's actual failure classification.
location: src/main/orky/orky-cli-runner.ts:20-40 (runOrkyCli, unmodified) + src/main/orky/orky-action-dispatcher.ts:215 (REQ-013 submit --json argv) + src/renderer/components/OrkyCaptureModal.tsx (no client-side cap, REQ-010); tests/main/orky-action-dispatcher-submit.test.ts TEST-471 (mocked runCli only)
resolution: Re-verified 2026-07-02 (rev-2, REQ-014): runOrkyCli now splits the spawn class from the elapsed-time class — isSpawnFailure (err.syscall startsWith 'spawn', orky-cli-runner.ts:29-31) and the synchronous try/catch (:63-68) both resolve {exitCode:null, timedOut:false} for a child that never ran, so the byte-unchanged mapCliRunToResult yields a DEFINITE cli-error/cli-unparseable, never the indeterminate cli-timeout. Traced the discriminant: abort (code 'ABORT_ERR', no syscall) and the timeout kill (killed:true, code null) fall through to timedOut:true, so TEST-509/510 stay green while TEST-507 (real 8MB argv sync-throw) and TEST-508 (mocked E2BIG callback) flip to definite; empty stdout parses to cli-unparseable '(0 bytes)' and parseDisabledRefusal (exitCode!==1) never misclassifies it.

claim: parseDisabledRefusal discriminates the DISTINCT feedback-disabled outcome purely on exitCode===1 and parsed.ok===false and parsed.mode==='noop'. That shape is exclusive to the disabled refusal only because of how the CURRENT C:/dev/Orky/plugin/feedback/feedback.js submitItem happens to be written (verified by reading the source: the http refusal returns mode:'http', the validation refusal returns mode:cfg.mode which is 'file' when enabled, and a genuine internal error propagates as an uncaught exception mapped by cli.js's outer catch to exit 2, never exit 1 with mode:'noop'). Nothing enforces this invariant across the repo boundary -- no shared schema, contract test, or stable error code ties Termhalla's dispatcher to Orky's plugin output shape. If a future Orky plugin release ever reuses mode:'noop' for a different exit-1 refusal, or a new noop-like state, Termhalla's dispatcher would silently misclassify it as the softer feedback-disabled non-dispatch message instead of a generic cli-error, and none of the mocked dispatcher tests (which fake runCli against today's assumption) would catch the drift, since they only assert the dispatcher's own reaction to a hand-constructed fixture, never the plugin's real behavior.
fix: Widen the discriminator so it depends on more than one loosely-coupled field (for example additionally require a stable substring/prefix of the disabled refusal's own error text, e.g. starts with 'feedback is disabled', rather than mode alone), and/or add a cross-repo fixture or documented contract test that pins the exact submitItem disabled-refusal shape so a future plugin change that breaks the assumption fails loudly instead of silently. Record the cross-repo dependency explicitly in the orky-action-dispatcher.ts header comment or docs/features/orky-action-dispatch.md.
location: src/main/orky/orky-action-dispatcher.ts:243-260 (parseDisabledRefusal) vs C:/dev/Orky/plugin/feedback/feedback.js:278-299 (submitItem)
note: ESC-001 residual (2026-07-02 re-verify): correctly documented as accepted — spec 'Review residuals' #2 records the cross-repo mode:'noop' shape coupling as a candidate for a future golden-fixture suite pinning the submitItem refusal shapes; also documented in REQ-013 + the dispatcher-doc updates. Left OPEN per instruction; not resolved by F12.

claim: Every free-text field that travels as its OWN raw (non-JSON-wrapped) argv element -- feature, escalationId, decision, evidence -- is defended by rejectFlagLike (rejects a leading --) before it can reach a CLI. projectRoot is the one exception: it flows straight from requireNonEmptyString into '--app', projectRoot in doSubmitWork/doResolveEscalation/doRecordHumanGate/doDriveStatus with no such check. Today this is NOT exploitable through F12's shipped UI: the only reachable root source is OrkyRootPicker, sourced from registry.roots(), and every registered root is produced by validateRegistryRoot which calls path.resolve() before storing it (verified in orky-registry.ts:136-151 addRoot and validate-root.ts) -- an OS-resolved absolute path can never literally start with '--' (it always starts with a drive letter or a slash). But this safety property is an UNENFORCED, undocumented invariant that F12 is the first renderer-reachable feature to actually depend on (submitWork previously had zero UI consumers, 0007 TEST-282), and 02-spec.md's own decision #4/REQ-004 explicitly earmarks a FUTURE pre-selected-root entry path (openOrkyCapture(root), for F10's OrkyPane inject gesture) that deliberately forgoes client-side membership pre-validation and would carry an even less-trusted string all the way to checkRoot before any structural check runs.
fix: Add the same rejectFlagLike('projectRoot', ...) guard already used for feature/escalationId/decision/evidence to all four validators in orky-action-validate.ts, for defense-in-depth, independent of whether resolve() currently happens to make the gap unreachable. This is an F7-owned file that REQ-013 pins BYTE-UNCHANGED for this feature, so F12 itself should not touch orky-action-validate.ts directly; record this as a flagged upstream gap (analogous to 02-spec.md's own Upstream inconsistencies flagged section) for a future F7-scoped change, and in the meantime keep the resolve()-always-absolute invariant documented as load-bearing wherever checkRoot/registry.roots() is described so a future change to root storage does not silently reopen this gap.
location: src/shared/orky-action-validate.ts:93-188 (projectRoot extracted via requireNonEmptyString in all four validators, never passed through rejectFlagLike, unlike feature/escalationId/decision/evidence) vs src/main/orky/orky-action-dispatcher.ts:215 (--app, projectRoot, raw argv element)
resolution: Re-verified 2026-07-02 (rev-2, REQ-013): rejectFlagLike('projectRoot', ...) now fires immediately after requireNonEmptyString in ALL FOUR validators (orky-action-validate.ts:98/:128/:162/:202), ahead of every later field so its CONV-001 message wins. TEST-512..517 pin all four + first-check ordering + pairwise-distinct messages; TEST-518 pins the OS-absolute non-false-positive (Windows drive path and POSIX path both accepted — a path.resolve()-d root can never start with '--').

location: src/renderer/components/OrkyCaptureModal.tsx:156-179 + 02-spec.md REQ-007/REQ-008
claim: The primary capture journey can end in a silent, irreversible write with no visible confirmation: (a) Enter in the title input submits immediately (REQ-007 row 2) with NO visible affordance anywhere - a user pressing Enter to move down to the detail field performs the durable inbox write and loses the chance to add the intended detail (no edit/undo path exists); (b) mod+Enter and Esc-discard are equally undiscoverable - the form carries no hint copy and the submit button no chord hint; (c) toastsEnabled defaults OFF (src/shared/types.ts:240 'toasts render only when strictly true (default off)'), so out of the box the D5 success toast never renders and the ONLY confirmation is the modal closing - after an accidental Enter the modal simply vanishes, telling a default-config user nothing about whether (or into which project's inbox) a half-formed item was captured. Each piece is individually spec-sanctioned (REQ-007 matrix; REQ-008/CONV-004 suppressible success); their combination makes the accidental-submit path silent end-to-end.
fix: Add a static keyboard-hint line to the form (static copy is permitted - the heading and 'Project:' label are already non-enumerated), e.g. 'Enter captures - Ctrl+Enter from anywhere - Esc discards'; and amend REQ-008 so the default-suppressed case has SOME attributable confirmation (e.g. a brief inline 'Captured' state before close, or the hint naming the destination inbox), so an accidental Enter is at least explained.
resolution: Re-verified 2026-07-02 (rev-2, REQ-002/REQ-007, ESC-001): the always-visible orky-capture-hint ('Enter captures · Ctrl+Enter captures from anywhere · Esc cancels', OrkyCaptureModal.tsx:270-272) makes the fast-capture + discard keys discoverable before the first accidental Enter, so the kept Enter-in-title capture is no longer a silent surprise; TEST-504 asserts the hint renders and names Enter/Esc. Success confirmation stays the CONV-004-suppressible pushToast (REQ-008) by decision — the hint gives the silent close its meaning.

location: src/renderer/components/OrkyCaptureModal.tsx:81 (aliveRef drop), :241 (cancel enabled in-flight) + 02-spec.md REQ-012 ('dropped without error')
claim: Closing the modal while a dispatch is in flight (Escape, the always-enabled Cancel button, or a backdrop click) discards the draft AND silently drops the settled outcome in both directions: if the write FAILS, the typed text is gone and the failure is reported nowhere renderer-side - the in-modal failure surface no longer exists and no error-kind toast is pushed, making this the one failure class in the feature with zero user-facing signal (against the spirit of CONV-004/CONV-015, and the exact silent-loss quick-capture exists to prevent); if it SUCCEEDS, a user who Escaped believing they cancelled has an item land in the project inbox with no disclosure that close cannot abort an in-flight write. Only the main-process audit records the outcome. Root cause is spec-phase: REQ-012 pins the settled result of an abandoned in-flight call as 'dropped without error'.
fix: Report the dropped settled outcome through the store-level chokepoint, which needs no live component: on failure pushToast(text, 'error') (never suppressible) naming the lost title; optionally the normal success toast on success. Alternatively hold/disable the close affordances during the bounded (max 15s) in-flight window. Requires a small REQ-012 amendment. propose CONV: When a surface owning a non-idempotent in-flight write is dismissed, the settled outcome MUST still be reported through a component-independent user-facing signal (error-kind for failures) - never dropped silently.
resolution: Re-verified 2026-07-02 (rev-2, REQ-009/REQ-012): a mid-flight-closed outcome now DETACHES without a component update yet is never dropped — OrkyCaptureModal.tsx:115-123 routes a failure to the never-suppressed error-kind pushToast (toasts-slice.ts:20) naming the lost title, success to the suppressible toast (:106-108). TEST-527 pins the detached failure toast enqueuing even with toastsEnabled false and exactly one dispatch. The narrower detached-toast honesty-class WORDING gap for the indeterminate kinds is tracked as the new FINDING-024.

location: tests/e2e/orky-capture.spec.ts + 04-tests.md suite map (REQ-009 -> TEST-492/506; REQ-006/REQ-012 mid-flight vectors)
claim: REQ-009 is pinned by shallow tests relative to its own acceptance: the acceptance demands one RENDER vector per errorKind (modal stays open, draft byte-preserved, data-error-kind correct, error text verbatim), explicitly including the http-refusal-rendered-as-cli-error distinctness vector and the indeterminate cli-timeout copy - but the shipped suite renders exactly ONE kind (feedback-disabled, TEST-506); every other kind exists only as source greps (TEST-492), which cannot catch a branch-routing or rendering bug. The e2e stub already has everything needed: mode.json can answer the http-refusal shape, and delayMs > 15000 would exercise cli-timeout end-to-end; one generic-kind vector would cover the six uniform kinds. Similarly, REQ-006/REQ-012's close-while-in-flight and unmount-mid-flight behaviors ('closing the modal while a dispatch is in flight MUST NOT issue another'; 'unmount during a pending mocked dispatch settles ... without a second call') are exercised nowhere behaviorally - TEST-505 only waits out the slow dispatch. The node-harness constraint justified structural pins where the e2e could not reach; these vectors it could.
fix: Loop back to the tests phase (a sanctioned amendment of the frozen e2e suite): add vectors for (a) a generic cli-error render (verbatim text, kind attr, draft preserved), (b) the http-refusal shape rendering as cli-error - distinct from feedback-disabled - and mentioning the control plane, (c) cli-timeout via delayMs>15000 asserting the indeterminate + duplicate-warning copy, (d) Escape during a slow in-flight dispatch asserting exactly one argv-log entry and no second dispatch.
note: ESC-001 residual, left OPEN per coordinator (2026-07-02 re-verify): confirmed the per-kind RENDER vectors now exist end-to-end in tests/e2e/orky-capture.spec.ts — TEST-525 (generic cli-error + http-refusal, verbatim, draft-preserved), TEST-526 (cli-timeout INDETERMINATE copy via delayMs>15s), TEST-527 (Escape during in-flight -> exactly one dispatch + detached failure toast). Substance addressed; status retained per instruction.

location: src/renderer/components/OrkyCaptureModal.tsx:55 (handlePickSelect) + :70 (failure cleared only at dispatch)
claim: The failure region persists across a root change: after a per-project failure (e.g. the feedback-disabled refusal for project A), choosing a different project via 'Change project' returns to the form still showing A's verbatim refusal beneath a target that now displays B - the rendered error mis-describes the CURRENT target until the next submit. 'failure' state is cleared only inside submitCapture, never when the root it described changes.
fix: Clear the failure state in handlePickSelect when a (different) root is chosen - the error's subject changed, so the stale verdict should not outlive it. Compatible with REQ-009 (draft preservation concerns title/detail/root, not the error region).
resolution: Consolidated into FINDING-008 (codex-cross-check filed the same stale-failure-region defect first; concurrent fan-out).

location: src/renderer/components/OrkyCaptureModal.tsx:170 (aria-modal) + src/renderer/components/Modal.tsx:83-90 (no focus containment)
claim: The capture dialog declares aria-modal="true" but the shared Modal substrate provides no focus containment: Tab from the Cancel button (the last control) walks focus out of the dialog into the backdropped app behind - including xterm panes that swallow Tab - and once focus leaves the dialog div, the Escape handler (attached to that div) no longer fires, dead-ending REQ-007's keyboard-only journey until a mouse backdrop click. Inherited from Modal.tsx (the F9 picker shares it), but F12 is now the third keyboard-first Orky surface on this seam and its REQ-007 matrix pins 'Escape anywhere in the form' plus a Tab order with no containment/wrap row.
fix: Add Tab containment at the Modal seam (wrap focus at the card boundary), or at minimum handle Escape at the backdrop/window level while a dialog is open. propose CONV: A dialog rendered with aria-modal="true" MUST contain Tab focus within the dialog (via the shared Modal substrate, never per-dialog copies), so the accessibility claim and the keyboard reality agree.
note: ESC-001 residual (2026-07-02 re-verify): correctly documented as accepted — spec 'Review residuals' #1 + proposed conv #8 record Tab containment as a shared Modal-seam gap across 20 dialogs, deferred, with Escape at the form root as the noted mitigation. Left OPEN per instruction; not resolved by F12.

location: src/renderer/components/OrkyCaptureModal.tsx:167-201 (card maxHeight 80vh with default overflow; textarea resize:vertical unbounded; error region pre-wrap unbounded)
claim: The form has no overflow containment: the card pins maxHeight '80vh' but neither the card nor the form column scrolls or clips, so a user-dragged textarea (resize:vertical with only a minHeight) or a long verbatim cli-error message (whiteSpace pre-wrap, no max height) grows the content past 80vh and spills visibly outside the card (default overflow visible), pushing the Capture/Cancel row off the card chrome. The picker, by contrast, contains its listbox with overflowY:auto. Pasting a 10k-line detail is fine (the textarea scrolls internally at rows=5) - the exposure is textarea resize and oversized error text.
fix: Bound the textarea resize with a CSS max-height and give the error region (or the form column) overflowY:auto with a max height, matching the picker's containment pattern.
resolution: Re-verified 2026-07-02 (rev-2): overflow is contained — the detail textarea is clamped maxHeight '30vh' + overflowY:'auto' (OrkyCaptureModal.tsx:214, a CSS clamp not a value cap, REQ-010 preserved) and the verbatim error region carries overflowY:'auto' + maxHeight '30vh' (:230), so neither a user-dragged textarea nor a long CLI message spills past the 80vh card or pushes the Capture/Cancel row off-chrome.

location: 02-spec.md REQ-006/REQ-012 acceptance (StrictMode vectors) vs tests/e2e/orky-capture.spec.ts:3-6 (frozen header claim) + 04-tests.md 'FINDING-004 / StrictMode' note
claim: The FINDING-004-repair acceptance ('the zero-dispatch-on-mount and single-flight vectors are exercised with the component wrapped in <StrictMode> ... STILL assert exactly zero/one') is executed by NO test and CANNOT be as written: the npm-test harness is environment:'node' with no DOM (cannot mount a React component at all — FINDING-004's resolution premise that 'React's dev build (which vitest uses) double-invokes effects' was false for this repo), and the substitute e2e runs Playwright against out/ — a PRODUCTION React bundle in which StrictMode's double mount/effect invocation is compiled out. So the frozen e2e header's claim 'every dispatch count here is measured against the real lifecycle ... <StrictMode>-wrapped' and 04-tests.md's '(b) the e2e zero/one dispatch counts measured against the shipped <StrictMode> tree' are false mechanism claims (the CONV-008/FINDING-007 class, now frozen into a test header). The genuinely load-bearing mitigation is only TEST-489's structural pin (no orkySubmitWork inside any useEffect callback) — sound — but the spec's frozen acceptance was substituted at the tests phase without a spec loopback/amendment, and the one StrictMode-active tree (npm run dev) is guarded by nothing but that structural pin.
fix: Amend REQ-006/REQ-012's acceptance (documented supersession) to name the sanctioned substitute — the TEST-489 structural pin plus production-e2e counts — explicitly recording that no StrictMode-active vector exists; correct the two false prose claims (orky-capture.spec.ts header, 04-tests.md) to state the e2e tree is a production build where StrictMode is inert. propose CONV: A StrictMode/dev-only-behavior acceptance vector MUST name a harness that actually runs React's development build — a production-build e2e does not exercise StrictMode semantics and must never be cited as doing so.
resolution: Re-verified 2026-07-02 (rev-2, FINDING-018): the false StrictMode mechanism claims are corrected — REQ-006 (spec:682-693) and REQ-012 (:917-919,928-929) state plainly that NO shipped harness runs React's dev build (unit harness is node-env/cannot mount; e2e drives the production out/ bundle with StrictMode inert), the class guard is the STRUCTURAL TEST-489 pin (orkySubmitWork in an event handler, never a useEffect), and the e2e header (orky-capture.spec.ts:6-12) + 04-tests were repaired to say the counts measure the real lifecycle but NOT StrictMode. Proposed conv #5 recorded; no artifact now claims a StrictMode-active vector.

location: src/renderer/components/OrkyCaptureModal.tsx:76-79 (the invoke-rejection catch)
claim: The catch around api.orkySubmitWork fabricates a result F7 never returned — {ok:false, dispatched:false, errorKind:'cli-error', error:String(err)} — and routes it through the same data-error-kind="cli-error" surface as F7's genuine mapped verdicts, under the DEFINITE copy 'Capture did not go through.' At the moment ipcRenderer.invoke rejects (a renderer/main transport failure — the dispatcher itself is total and never throws), the dispatch fate is UNKNOWN: the request may have reached the dispatcher and completed the write, so presenting a definite non-capture is the same indeterminacy misstatement CONV-015/REQ-009 forbids for cli-timeout — albeit on a near-unreachable defensive path. Automation/tests keying on data-error-kind also cannot distinguish this renderer-synthesized kind from an F7-mapped cli-error, softening REQ-008's 'keyed ONLY on the result's own fields' discipline at the margin.
fix: Route the catch through the indeterminate presentation (the cli-timeout copy class: may or may not have been captured; retrying may create a duplicate) or a renderer-scoped kind (e.g. 'ipc-failure') so a synthesized verdict is never byte-identical to an F7-mapped one.
resolution: Re-verified 2026-07-02 (rev-2, REQ-014/REQ-009): the api.orkySubmitWork invoke-rejection catch now synthesizes the renderer-scoped errorKind 'ipc-failure' (OrkyCaptureModal.tsx:97), never an F7 literal, and the in-modal path renders it through the INDETERMINATE copy class shared with cli-timeout (:246-257 'may or may not have been captured' + duplicate warning). data-error-kind='ipc-failure' (:228) is byte-distinct from an F7-mapped 'cli-error'. (In-modal only; the detached-toast wording for this kind is FINDING-024.)

location: 02-spec.md REQ-011 ("F12 MUST keep TEST-282, TEST-433, TEST-434, TEST-362/363, TEST-196..203, TEST-274, TEST-281, TEST-290, TEST-325..329, ... passing BYTE-UNCHANGED" + "The ONLY frozen pins F12 supersedes are the SIX enumerated") vs tests/shared/orky-action-validate.test.ts (diff: :38-44 comment + :322 it-description; assertions byte-unchanged) vs .orky/features/0012-quick-capture-inbox/04-tests.md:106-110 (TASK-002) vs findings.json FINDING-007 (status: open) vs .orky/conventions.md CONV-019/CONV-023
claim: REQ-011's frozen text in 02-spec.md is now literally false and is an uncorrected CONV-019/CONV-023 violation. REQ-011 states TEST-290 must stay "passing BYTE-UNCHANGED" and that "the ONLY frozen pins F12 supersedes are the SIX enumerated" (TEST-237/238/293/298/300/267 + the :37 header comment) -- TEST-290 is explicitly excluded from that list. But the shipped tests/shared/orky-action-validate.test.ts diff (git-verified) modifies both the file's :38-44 scope comment and TEST-290's it()-description string (assertions untouched) -- a real, non-empty diff to a test REQ-011 pins as byte-unchanged and explicitly does NOT enumerate as superseded. This is FINDING-007 (spec-gate, devils-advocate, still status:'open') materializing exactly as predicted: FINDING-007 traced the root cause to REQ-011's own "Grep patterns and scope used" section, which states revision 1's argv-pin grep (work.request|--payload|--type) was scoped to tests/main/** ONLY -- never repo-wide or tests/**, as CONV-023 requires ("the pattern MUST target the pinned literal value repo-wide -- never a sweep of already-known files") -- which is why it missed tests/shared's stale --payload mechanism prose. The tests phase (04-tests.md TASK-002:106-110) DID transparently document and repair the prose ("FINDING-007 ... repaired here under the same CONV-019 protocol"; "Assertions byte-untouched; TEST-290 stays green") -- but CONV-019 requires that "the consumer feature's SPEC MUST retire/supersede the guard atomically in the same change, at the tests phase". 02-spec.md's REQ-011 was never revised to add this seventh entry or retract its byte-unchanged claim for TEST-290, so the FROZEN, gating spec document still asserts something the shipped code contradicts, and FINDING-007 remains open in the ledger despite its underlying issue being patched out-of-band by the test-designer rather than through a sanctioned spec revision + re-gate.
fix: Loopback to spec (or a doc-sync amendment) to revise 02-spec.md REQ-011: either (a) add TEST-290 as a documented seventh, prose-only CONV-019 supersession (mirroring 04-tests.md TASK-002's own language) and drop it from the "stays byte-unchanged" list, or (b) explicitly record the accepted prose-only exception inline in REQ-011. Re-run the CONV-023 grep repo-wide (not scoped to tests/main/**) to confirm no other stale mechanism reference was missed by the same under-scoped pattern. Mark FINDING-007 resolved once 02-spec.md matches reality, citing the 04-tests.md TASK-002 repair as evidence. propose CONV: A frozen-pin supersession performed at the tests phase under CONV-019 (including a prose-only one triggered by a spec-gate finding) MUST be reflected back into the spec document's own enumeration in the same change -- a test-phase artifact documenting a repair is not a substitute for correcting the frozen spec's own MUST-level claim.
resolution: Re-verified 2026-07-02 (rev-2, FINDING-020): REQ-011 now enumerates the SEVEN superseded pins (six F7 dispatcher-pin amendments + entry-7 prose-only: TEST-290's it-title + the validate suite's :38-44 scope comment) and scopes TEST-290's byte-unchanged claim to its ASSERTIONS only (spec:864,245). I independently re-ran the argv-pin grep (work.request / --payload / --type) REPO-WIDE over tests/** and fully classified the universe: the only stale-mechanism prose was orky-action-validate.test.ts:42 + :324, now repaired to name submitWork's --json element (resolveEscalation legitimately keeps --payload); every other hit is a current submit pin, a still-live resolveEscalation emit pin, a banned-argv assertion (TEST-470), or the mechanism-agnostic runner vector. REQ-011's completeness claim holds.

location: src/renderer/components/CommandPalette.tsx (diff: the pre-existing currentCwd useMemo, unrelated to feature 0012's own logic) vs tests/renderer/orky-capture-structure.test.ts:285 (TEST-495: `const guardIdx = palette.indexOf('if (!activeId) return')`)
claim: The F12 diff to CommandPalette.tsx contains one hunk with no functional connection to the capture feature: inside the pre-existing currentCwd useMemo, `if (!activeId) return ''; const ws = workspaces[activeId]` was rewritten to `const ws = activeId ? workspaces[activeId] : undefined` (falling through to the existing `if (!ws) return ''`). Verified behaviorally identical by trace (both paths return '' when activeId is falsy; both compute the same ws when truthy) -- but the only reason for the edit is to dodge a collision in the FROZEN TEST-495 (orky-capture-structure.test.ts:285): `palette.indexOf('if (!activeId) return')` is an unanchored, whole-file .indexOf that, before this edit, matched the currentCwd useMemo's early return FIRST (it is a literal string-prefix match, and appears earlier in the file than the new capture-orky-work case), producing a guardIdx pointing at unrelated code rather than the actual command-activation guard the test means to locate -- which would have made the real 'capture handled before the activeId guard' assertion fail for a reason unrelated to the property being tested. Removing the only other occurrence of the substring makes TEST-495 locate the correct (later) guard. This workaround is invisible: nothing in the diff comments on WHY an unrelated hook was reshaped, so a future contributor who reverts it to the more idiomatic early-return (it reads as an unmotivated complication) will silently break a frozen structural test in a different file/feature, for a reason that has nothing to do with whatever they touched.
fix: Add a one-line comment at the currentCwd useMemo noting it was reshaped to avoid colliding with TEST-495's indexOf('if (!activeId) return') scan (orky-capture-structure.test.ts:285), so a future revert doesn't blindly reintroduce the collision. Separately, TEST-495's locator is fragile by construction (a whole-file, unanchored .indexOf substring match) and will collide again with ANY future 'if (!activeId) return' text introduced earlier in the file for an unrelated reason; the next time this test is touched, anchor the search (scope it to the activate()/switch function body, or match a more specific marker such as the adjacent 'Commands below need an active workspace' comment). propose CONV: A frozen structural test that locates a code site via a whole-file .indexOf() string search MUST anchor the search (a scoped substring, a preceding/following marker, or a bounded regex) rather than a bare literal that can collide with unrelated occurrences elsewhere in the same file.
resolution: Re-verified 2026-07-02 (rev-2, FINDING-021): the currentCwd useMemo carries an explicit comment naming the collision (CommandPalette.tsx:35-40: 'NOTE (FINDING-021 / TEST-495, tests/renderer/orky-capture-structure.test.ts:285) ... Do NOT revert to the early return'), so a revert cannot blindly reintroduce the unanchored-indexOf collision. Proposed conv #6 recorded.

location: 02-spec.md REQ-004 acceptance vs traceability.json REQ-004:[TEST-481,TEST-482,TEST-505] vs tests/renderer/orky-capture-slice.test.ts (TEST-481/482, slice-state only) vs tests/e2e/orky-capture.spec.ts:28-29
claim: REQ-004's acceptance enumerates five distinct testable behaviors for the D4 pre-selected-root path: (1) `openOrkyCapture('C:\\Mixed\\Case Root')` opens the form (never the picker) showing that exact string, (2) the dispatched request's projectRoot is byte-equal to it (spy), (3) an untracked pre-selected root submits, receives the mocked root-not-allowed result, and renders its verbatim error with the draft preserved, (4) the change-root button still opens the picker, and (5) a slice-level test covers openOrkyCapture() vs openOrkyCapture(root) state. Only (5) is actually verified: TEST-481/482 (orky-capture-slice.test.ts) test the STATE shape returned by calling the slice function directly and never render OrkyCaptureModal. (1)-(3) require a component actually mounted with a non-null initialRoot -- and no such render exists anywhere in the shipped suite: the vitest unit harness is node-env/no-jsdom by established project convention (orky-capture-structure.test.ts:1-13), so it cannot mount React at all, and the e2e suite's own header comment (orky-capture.spec.ts:28-29) explicitly and honestly discloses the gap: 'The D4 openOrkyCapture(root) pre-selected SKIP itself has no shipped caller until F10 -- pinned at the slice seam, TEST-481.' Both shipped invocation paths (chord, palette) call openOrkyCapture() with zero arguments, so e2e literally cannot reach the initialRoot!=null branch without a test-only backdoor, which was not added. The risk is mitigated -- once root is set, CaptureForm/submit/failure-rendering are the SAME code already exercised via the picker-selected path, and the initialRoot-skip logic itself is two trivial useState initializers -- but the acceptance criteria as WRITTEN in the frozen 02-spec.md are not met by any test, and the deferral is disclosed only in test-phase/e2e-header prose; 02-spec.md's own REQ-004 acceptance text gives no indication that (1)-(3) are deferred to F10.
fix: Either (a) add a lightweight structural pin (source-scan, consistent with this repo's established node-env pattern) asserting OrkyCaptureModal honors a non-null initialRoot by skipping the picker (e.g., that `picking` initializes from `initialRoot === null` and the JSX conditionally renders OrkyRootPicker only when picking), closing the coverage gap for (1) without needing jsdom/e2e; or (b) amend 02-spec.md REQ-004's acceptance text itself to explicitly say clauses (1)-(3) are deferred until F10 supplies a real caller (mirroring the honest disclosure already present in tests/e2e/orky-capture.spec.ts:28-29 and TEST-481's docstring), so the frozen spec doesn't imply coverage that doesn't exist. Either way, close the silent gap between what REQ-004's acceptance promises and what 04-tests.md actually delivered.
resolution: Re-verified 2026-07-02 (rev-2, FINDING-022): REQ-004's acceptance is honestly scoped (spec:620-636) — verified NOW: (a) the openOrkyCapture()/openOrkyCapture(root) slice test, (b) a structural source-scan pin that OrkyCaptureModal honors a non-null initialRoot by skipping the picker (picking = initialRoot===null, OrkyCaptureModal.tsx:35; OrkyRootPicker rendered only under picking), (c) reinvoke-while-open in REQ-006; the render/dispatch clauses (1)-(4) are explicitly recorded as acceptance debt INHERITED BY F10's e2e in the change that ships the first real openOrkyCapture(root) caller, never presented as covered by F12.

location: NUL (repo root, tracked; git show HEAD:NUL is 0 bytes; git log --oneline -1 -- NUL -> 796c11b 'feat(orky-0009): native OrkyPane pane kind')
claim: The repo tracks a file literally named 'NUL' at the root (0 bytes, introduced in commit 796c11b, feature 0009 -- predates and is unrelated to F12's diff). 'NUL' is a Windows-reserved device name (like CON/PRN/AUX/COM1-9/LPT1-9); Win32 file APIs intercept any path component matching it (case-insensitively, with or without an extension) and redirect it to the null device instead of treating it as an ordinary filename. This makes the file effectively un-materializable through normal Windows file APIs, breaking git operations that need to write it to a real path on checkout -- surfaced during this feature's (F12) build as git stash/checkout failures on Windows. It is pre-existing repo debt, not introduced by this diff, but it actively harms Windows-based contributors to a project that is developed on Windows (this environment), and will keep resurfacing on every future feature's build until removed or renamed.
fix: `git rm NUL` (or `git mv NUL <a non-reserved name>` if the zero-byte file serves a real purpose, e.g. a sentinel/placeholder) in a follow-up housekeeping change; add a pre-commit or CI check rejecting NUL/CON/PRN/AUX/COM1-9/LPT1-9 (case-insensitive, with or without extension) as a path component so a Windows-reserved name can never be re-added. Not blocking for F12 (unrelated, pre-existing), but worth a tracked follow-up since it degrades the Windows dev loop this repo is actively used on.
note: ESC-001 residual (2026-07-02 re-verify): correctly documented as accepted — spec 'Review residuals' #3 records the tracked NUL file as pre-existing Windows-reserved-name repo debt to be removed in a separate hygiene commit, outside F12 scope. Left OPEN per instruction; not resolved by F12.

location: src/renderer/components/OrkyCaptureModal.tsx:121 (the detached-failure pushToast) vs 02-spec.md REQ-009 'Close-while-in-flight' (~:803-805)
claim: The FINDING-013 detached-toast fix contradicts REQ-009's own MUST for the indeterminate kinds. REQ-009 (rev-2) requires the settled-after-close failure toast to carry 'the kind's own honesty class (indeterminate wording for cli-timeout/ipc-failure, definite otherwise)' (CONV-015), but OrkyCaptureModal.tsx:121 pushes uniform DEFINITE wording for every kind: `Capture failed for "${title}" — ${fail.error}`. When the modal is closed mid-flight and the dispatch then TIMES OUT (the 15s runner timeout) or the invoke REJECTS (ipc-failure), the detached toast asserts 'Capture failed' — a definite non-capture — for an outcome whose write may in fact have completed (the unref()'d child can still finish; the dispatcher may have applied the request before the transport failed). A user reads 'failed' and retries, creating exactly the duplicate CONV-015/REQ-009 forbid. The in-modal render path (:246-257) correctly distinguishes the indeterminate class; only the detached toast collapses it. Untested: TEST-527 exercises only the DEFINITE feedback-disabled detached case and asserts only that the toast names the title, not the honesty class, so the gap is unpinned.
fix: In the !stillMounted branch, choose the toast wording by fail.kind: for cli-timeout/ipc-failure use indeterminate phrasing matching the in-modal copy class (the item may or may not have been captured; retrying may create a duplicate), keeping the definite 'Capture failed' only for the definite kinds. Add an e2e vector (delayMs>15s, close mid-flight) asserting the detached cli-timeout toast uses indeterminate wording and never the word 'failed'.
resolution: Fixed (coordinator, tests loopback): the detached close-in-flight toast now branches on fail.kind — cli-timeout/ipc-failure get indeterminate wording (may still have been captured; retrying may duplicate), matching the in-modal region and REQ-009/CONV-015; feedback-disabled and other kinds keep definite copy. Pinned by TEST-529 (new structural test); TEST-527 stays green.
